rulings.Case Title: WORKERS COMPENSATION SOLUTIONS LLC VS. ABM SERVICES INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hrg On Petition To Confirm Arb Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, September 12, 2014, Line 3, PETITIONER WORKERS COMPENSATION SOLUTIONS LLC's Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award Workers Compensation Solutions, LLC's Petition to Confirm Arbitration Award is granted. ABM Services, Inc.'s Petition to Vacate the Arbitration Award is denied. ABM fails to show that the award was procured by fraud, that the arbitrator exceeded his powers, or that its rights were substantially prejudiced by a refusal to postpone the hearing to hear material evidence. See CCP 1286.2(a). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: MR WIRE COMMUNICATIONS DBA MR WIRE VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, September 12, 2014, Line 5, CROSS DEFENDANT ROSENDIN ELECTRIC, INC.'s DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT Sustained without leave to amend. The liability alleged in the underlying complaint arises from the allegation that Defendant Regents of the University of California provided Plaintiff Mr. Wire with a defective design; specifically, that the plans provided by Regents had an incorrect scale. (Cross-Defendant Rosedin's Request for Judicial Notice, Ex. B, Second Amended Complaint, pars. 7-10, 30-41.) Cross-Defendant Rosendin cannot, as a matter of law, be obligated to indemnify or defend Regents against Mr. Wire's claims, per Cal. Civ. Code sec. 2782(a) and paragraphs 15.1.1-15.1.2 of the subcontract, attached as Exhibit D to the cross-complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT GOUVEIA et al VS. JAMES LITTRELL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers Of Plaintiffs To Defendants' Second Set Of Special Interrogatories And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 12, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT JAMES LITTRELL, LITTRELL, J., DBA RUBY LIVING, LLC, INTERIOR MOVES, LLC Motion To Compel Answers Of Plaintiffs To Defendants' Second Set Of Special Interrogatories And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The defendants' motion is GRANTED. The plaintiffs failed to respond to any of the interrogatories or ask for an extension of time to respond. All objections are waived. The plaintiffs are to serve code compliant responses within thirty (30) days. The defendant's request for sanctions is GRANTED. Plaintiffs shall pay defendants $855.00 within thirty (30) days. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@dplolaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SEAN NICHOLSON et al VS. HOUSE OF AIR, LLC et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, September 12, 2014, Line 8, CROSS DEFENDANT SHAWN NICHOLSON's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT Sustained in part and overruled in part. (CCP 430.10(e) and (f).) The demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to the 1st Cause of Action ("COA") for equitable indemnity. The Cross-Complaint ("X-C") does not adequately plead facts establishing that Cross-Defendant is jointly and severally liable to Plaintiff. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the 2nd COA for contribution as unopposed. The demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to the 3rd COA for declaratory relief for Cross-Complainant to plead facts clarifying how the alleged indemnity arises; i.e.: either by virtue of contract or under equitable principles. The demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to the 4th COA for breach of contract. Cross-Complainant may be able to state a COA that Cross-Defendant breached Exhibit B. The demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to the 5th COA for express indemnity. As pled, it is unclear how Cross-Complainant is entitled to express indemnity. The demurrer to the 6th COA for intentional fraud is overruled. The X-C adequately pleads the elements of fraud. The demurrer to the 7th COA for negligent misrepresentation is overruled. The X-C adequately pleads the elements of this COA. The demurrer to the 8th and 9th COAs for intentional and negligent interference with prospective economic relations is overruled. Liberally construing the X-C, as the Court must on demurrer, the X-C adequately pleads the elements of these COAs, including an existing economic relationship. The Court did not consider the Reply Declaration of Gregory M. Sheffer in ruling on this demurrer. A demurrer may only be used to challenge defects that appear on the face of the pleading or from matters judicially noticeable. (Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: CRAIG K MARTIN VS. DOES 1-50, INCLUSIVE
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, September 12, 2014, Line 2, PLAINTIFF CRAIG MARTIN Motion For Discovery. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The plaintiff's motion is DENIED. The motion was filed pursuant to CCP § 425.16(g) while the special motion to strike pursuant to CCP § 425.16 was under submission. That motion subsequently was granted. Thus, this motion is moot. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@dplolaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: MINOO SULTAN VS. NEW MARINA REAL ESTATE SOLUTIONS, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, September 12, 2014, Line 9, CROSS DEFENDANT MINOO SULTAN's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT The demurrer is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. Defendant has leave to plead an actual controversy within the meaning of CCP 1060. The cross-complainant appears to seek a declaration that it is entitled to its fees and costs if it prevails in the action. Pursuant to CRC 3.1700 and CRC 3.1702, cross-complainant shall obtain that relief by costs a memorandum and/or a noticed motion. The cross-complaint as presently pleaded is unnecessary and the court exercises its discretion pursuant to CCP 1061 to sustain the demurrer with leave to amend. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES D SUMNER et al VS. ARA AROUSTAMIAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, September 12, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANTS KARAPET BADALYAN and ARAM VARDANYAN's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS Motion denied. A defendant may move to quash service of summons on or before the last day of his or her time to plead. (CCP § 418.10(a).) Defendant's Motion was filed and served after that time had expired, and has not made a showing of good cause for the Court to extend the time to file. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)
